info.title: Nexus Exchange API
info.description: Perpetual futures exchange with HMAC API key authentication, tiered rate limiting, and real-time WebSocket streaming across 32 markets.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description: EVM wallet sign-in and API key management

tags.name: Agents
tags.description: Agent key registration and management (EIP-712 signed, no session token required)

post.description: Submit an EIP-191 personal_sign signature to receive a session token. The session token is used to create and manage API keys via /keys endpoints. For trading, use HMAC API keys instead. Session tokens expire after 24 hours.
Create a new HMAC API key for the authenticated wallet. Returns the secret once — it is never stored or shown again. Requires a session token (Bearer) from POST /auth/login.

get.description: Returns key IDs and tiers for all keys owned by the authenticated wallet. Secrets are not included.

delete.description: Delete a key you own. Cannot delete keys owned by other wallets.

404.description: Key not found or not owned by you

post.description:
Register a new agent key for your wallet. An agent is an Ethereum-derived keypair that can sign trading requests on your behalf without exposing your main wallet. The registration is authorized by an EIP-712 signature from the wallet that will own the agent — no session token required.

EIP-712 domain: `{ name: 'NexusExchange', version: '1', chainId: <testnet chain id> }`.
Typed data type: `RegisterAgent { address agent, uint256 expiresAt, uint256 nonce }`.

400.description: Bad request: bad_wallet, bad_agent, expiry_out_of_range [1 d, 90 d from now], or invalid_json

401.description: signature_invalid or signer_mismatch — the EIP-712 signature did not recover to the claimed wallet

409.description: duplicate_agent — the agent address is already registered to this wallet

get.description: Returns all non-expired agent keys registered to the authenticated wallet.

delete.description: Immediately revoke an agent key. Any in-flight requests signed by the revoked agent will be rejected after this call returns.

404.description: Agent not found or not owned by you

post.description: Legacy endpoint. Prefer POST /ws/token which supports both HMAC keys and registered agents. Returns a short-lived (60s), single-use token for the public /stream endpoint.

Returns a short-lived (60s), single-use token bound to the authenticated account. Pass it as `?token=TOKEN` when upgrading to `GET /ws`. Supports HMAC keys, registered agent keys, and session tokens (Bearer). The token encodes the account identity so per-account channels (orders, fills, positions, balances) are automatically scoped to the connected wallet.

get.description: Returns market parameters for all perpetual futures markets including tick size, lot size, margin rates, and maximum leverage.
Returns last trade price, 24h volume, and trade count for all markets.

200.description: Volume and price summaries for all markets.
Current order book for the market.

get.description: Returns candlestick data as arrays: [timestamp, open, high, low, close, volume].

200.description: Funding rate history for the market.

get.summary: Get market status and halt info (v0.21)
get.description: Returns current market status including halt state from ADL exhaustion. Halted markets reject new orders with ExchangeError::MarketHalted.

get.summary: Get ADL settlement history for a market (v0.21)
get.description: Returns up to `limit` ADL settlement events for the market, most recent first. Populated when the insurance fund is depleted and auto-deleveraging closes opposite-side positions to absorb bad debt.

200.description: ADL settlement history for the market.

get.summary: Get ADL events touching an account (v0.21)
get.description: Returns ADL settlements where the specified address was either the bankrupt target or one of the counterparties whose position was closed.

200.description: Current mark price for the market.

400.description: Validation error (insufficient margin, invalid tick size, etc.)

parameters.name: market_id
parameters.description: Cancel only orders on this market

post.description: Submit multiple orders in one request. Orders are processed sequentially and non-atomically: an early order consuming margin can cause a later order in the same batch to fail, and per-order failures do not abort the batch. The response array preserves request order with a per-order success or error result.

200.description: Account summary for the authenticated caller.

post.description: Credit synthetic USDX to the authenticated account, up to a per-API-key daily allowance (default 500 USDX, resets at midnight UTC). `amount` is a decimal string; omit it to claim the full remaining daily allowance. Returns 429 with code `daily_limit_exceeded` once the allowance is used up, and 403 with code `credits_frozen` while crediting is administratively frozen. This is a testnet faucet: the credited USDX is synthetic and carries no real-world value.

example.message: USDX crediting is temporarily frozen by an administrator. Existing balances remain tradeable.

429.description: Daily credit allowance exhausted (resets at midnight UTC)

example.message: daily USDX credit allowance reached for this API key

get.description: Returns the authenticated caller's rate limit tier, effective request ceiling, remaining requests, and reset timestamp — the same data surface as the X-RateLimit-* response headers, exposed as a queryable resource. This endpoint does not consume a rate limit token, so it can be polled freely to self-manage request pacing without depleting the caller's budget. For HMAC API keys with a per-key rate, the reported values are the binding minimum of the key bucket and the owner bucket. For unlimited-tier callers (gateway keys), `limit`, `remaining`, and `reset_at_ms` are null.

200.description: Current rate-limit status for the caller.

get.description: Returns up to 1000 fills for the authenticated account, newest first. Fills are trade executions resulting from order matches — each fill carries the matched price, quantity, fee, and whether it was the taker or maker side.
Returns up to 100 withdrawal records for the authenticated account, newest first.

WebSocket endpoint for both public market data and per-account private channels. Requires a token from POST /ws/token:
```
wss://host/ws?token=YOUR_TOKEN
```
Tokens are single-use and expire after 60 seconds.

Every client→server and server→client message is a JSON envelope tagged with an `op` field. Subscribe to one channel per message:
```json
{"op": "subscribe", "channel": "trades", "market": "BTC-USDX-PERP"}
{"op": "subscribe", "channel": "fills"}
```
Unsubscribe with `{"op": "unsubscribe", "channel": "...", "market": "..."}`.

**Public channels** (the token is always required to upgrade): `trades`, `book`, `candles` — each requires a `market` field.

**Per-account channels** (scoped to the wallet that minted the token): `orders`, `fills`, `positions`, `balances`.

**Server messages:**
- `{"op": "subscribed", "channel": "fills", "market": null, "seq_at_join": 42}` — subscribe ack; `seq_at_join` is the channel's current sequence at attach time, used as the reconnect cursor.
- `{"op": "unsubscribed", "channel": "fills", "market": null}` — unsubscribe ack.
- `{"op": "event", "channel": "fills", "market": null, "seq": 43, "payload": {...}}` — a delivered event; `seq` is monotonic per channel.
- `{"op": "out_of_sync", "channel": "fills", "market": null, "oldest_seq": 100}` — the requested `since` cursor fell behind the replay buffer; refetch state over REST and resubscribe with a fresh cursor.
- `{"op": "error", "message": "..."}` — invalid op, unknown channel, or bad message format.

**Reconnection:** tokens are single-use — mint a new one via POST /ws/token before reconnecting. To resume a channel without gaps, pass the last `seq` you received:
```json
{"op": "subscribe", "channel": "fills", "since": 42}
```

Legacy public-only WebSocket endpoint. Prefer GET /ws which also supports per-account channels. Requires a token from POST /ws-tokens. Unlike /ws, the protocol is a single untagged subscribe message listing channels:
```json
{"subscribe": ["trades:*", "book:BTC-USDX-PERP", "stats"]}
```
Channels: `trades:*` / `trades:{market_id}`, `book:*` / `book:{market_id}`, `stats`.

put.description: Assign a rate-limit tier to an address. Invalidates the existing rate-limit bucket so the new cap takes effect immediately.

get.description: Returns all addresses with non-default tier assignments.

delete.description: Remove a tier override, reverting the address to the default Pro tier.

get.description: No authentication required. Used by Fly.io health checks and uptime monitors.

hmacAuth.name: X-API-Key
hmacAuth.description:
HMAC API key authentication. Send three headers:
- `X-API-Key`: your key ID (e.g. `nx_a1b2c3...`)
- `X-Timestamp`: current time in unix milliseconds
- `X-Signature`: `hex(hmac_sha256(secret, timestamp + "\n" + METHOD + "\n" + path + "\n" + query + "\n" + sha256_hex(body)))`

Timestamp must be within 30 seconds of server time.

bearerAuth.description: Session token from POST /auth/login. Used only for API key management (/keys endpoints).

adminAuth.description: Admin secret (ADMIN_SECRET environment variable). Used for tier management and service control.

Unauthorized.description: Authentication failed. All 401 responses return the same opaque body to prevent information leakage.

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded. Check X-RateLimit-* headers and Retry-After.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Requests per second allowed for your tier

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix timestamp when the limit resets

message.description: Must be exactly: "Sign in to Nexus Exchange"

token.description: Session token (64-char hex). Use as Bearer token for /keys endpoints.

last_trade_price.description: Last trade price ("what the market is trading at"). NOT the mark; the engine-derived mark is exposed separately.

status.description: v0.21: halted when ADL pool exhausted

halted_at.description: Unix ms timestamp when market was halted

adl_event_count.description: Cumulative ADL settlement events for this market

AdlClosureRecord.description: One counterparty's forced closure within an ADL settlement.

account_id.description: 0x-prefixed address of the counterparty whose position was closed

settlement_amount.description: Decimal amount charged to the counterparty

AdlEventRecord.description: Single ADL settlement (insurance fund depleted → counterparty closures). v0.21.

markPrice.description: Engine-derived mark price (oracle + premium-index), falling back to the last trade until the first mark-price poll lands. The raw last trade is carried by `last`.

OrderBook.description: CCXT-compatible order book. Bids/asks are [price, amount] arrays.

amount.description: Synthetic USDX to credit (decimal string). Omit to claim the full remaining daily allowance.
USDX credited by this request (decimal string).

credited_today.description: Total USDX credited to this API key so far today (decimal string).

daily_limit.description: Per-API-key daily credit allowance in USDX (decimal string).

limit.description: Maximum requests per second. Also the burst capacity — the token bucket holds one second's worth of tokens — so `remaining` never exceeds it. Null for the unlimited tier.

remaining.description: Requests that can be made right now before throttling (tokens currently in the bucket). Null for the unlimited tier.

reset_at_ms.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the bucket refills back to `limit`; `0` when it is already full. Null for the unlimited tier.

agent.description: Agent Ethereum address (0x-prefixed, 20 bytes) derived from the agent keypair

expires_at.description: Expiry as Unix ms. Optional — defaults to now+30d. Must be in [now+1d, now+90d].

nonce.description: Monotonic nonce. Use the current Unix timestamp in ms as a safe starting value.

signature.description: EIP-712 signature over RegisterAgent{agent, expiresAt, nonce} from the wallet private key (0x-prefixed)

label.description: Optional human-readable label for the agent (e.g. 'my-bot')

Fill.description: A single trade execution for the authenticated account

Decimal.description: Arbitrary-precision decimal serialized as a string (lossless). Parse with a decimal type, never a float.
